tactical_shape: Solid team performance behind Alex Isak and Viktor Gyökeres
Relied on quick transitions/breaks rather than prolonged possession, with Gyökeres instrumental in the build-up
Defensive support functioned well enough to allow the forwards to show their quality
standout_players: Yasin Ayari - scored twice, including the opener and the fifth; one of Sweden's key attacking threats
Viktor Gyökeres - instrumental in the first goal, created the second, won/tackled for the third, and scored Sweden's third; influential night
Alexander Isak - assisted Gyökeres for the second goal and played Svanberg in for the fourth; showed his quality
Mattias Svanberg - scored the fourth after coming off the bench
Victor Lindelöf - provided the ball over the top for the opener
fatigue_injuries: Graham Potter was bleeding from an ear injury he was unable to explain
home_read.momentum: Potter's Sweden are continuing a remarkable renewal and look confident, with the win reinforcing their World Cup place and upward momentum.

tactical_shape: Did not start by trying to kill the game or waste time; there was an actual attempt to play football
Showed some creative endeavour and were willing to attack rather than purely spoil
Briefly had a spell after half-time where a comeback seemed possible
standout_players: Omar Rekik - headed in Tunisia's only goal from Hannibal Mejbri's cross
Hannibal Mejbri - provided the right-wing cross for the goal
away_read.momentum: A self-destructive display dominated by individual mistakes leaves Tunisia with an implosion to learn from and little positive momentum.
